description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a cylindrical body and domed top, featuring three prominent hose connection outlets spaced around its upper section. The hydrant is constructed from robust metal, with visible signs of weathering and dirt accumulation, especially near the base. Each outlet is capped, and two of the caps are secured with metal chains to prevent loss. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut, used for opening the valve to release water. The base is flanged and slightly wider, with recessed sections and bolt holes for mounting to the ground. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the surface.
description: A weathered red metal fire hydrant with three hose outlets, chained caps, and a domed top with an operating nut.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main hose outlet and cap with attached chain, as well as the domed top and central operating nut.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting two hose outlets, both with chained caps, and the side profile of the hydrant's cylindrical body.
description_view_2: Rear-side view displaying the third hose outlet and cap, with chains visible, and the flanged base with mounting recesses.
description_view_3: Back view showing the hydrant's smooth rear surface, the side outlet, and the absence of any visible markings or additional features.
